steps.title: Complete the comparison
steps.content: The phrase compares US Hispanics and non-Hispanic white counterparts while saying they are similarly stressed. The word "stressed" completes the comparison and is followed immediately by the verb phrase "is that," so no punctuation is needed after it.

steps.title: Reject unnecessary punctuation
steps.content: Choice B adds a comma that incorrectly separates the subject phrase from "is." Choice C uses a semicolon where the following words are not an independent clause. Choice D uses a colon where no explanation or list is being introduced at that point.

steps.title: Leave the phrase unpunctuated
steps.content: The unpunctuated word keeps the comparison connected to the rest of the sentence. The answer is A.
